reasoning: The resolution window is exactly 33-34 days (May 13 to June 14). Both sides appear to be in a prolonged diplomatic standoff following retaliatory tariffs in February and the cancellation of a previously scheduled call. As of mid-April, there were "no existing plans" for a call, and Trump noted he is in "no rush" to speak. Xi is also likely averse to initiating contact in a way that suggests he is caving to tariff pressure. Historically, US-China presidential phone calls occur perhaps 1-3 times a year. The base rate for a call in any random one-month period is around 10-25%, but given the current freeze and lack of an apparent catalyst scheduled for this specific mid-May to mid-June timeframe, the probability is lower than average. I estimate a 12% chance.
batch_queries: Will Donald Trump and Xi Jinping speak on the phone with each other before June 15, 2025?
